meta.name: AHS Adult Hope Scale
meta.description: The AHS is a 12-item self-report scale (8 scored items, 4 fillers) that assesses an individual’s level of hope. Hope comprises two dimensions: Pathways thinking (finding ways to achieve goals) and Agency thinking (motivation to pursue goals). It is a core concept in positive psychology.
meta.author: Snyder, Harris, Anderson, Holleran, Irving, Sigmon, et al. (1991)
meta.reference: Snyder CR, Harris C, Anderson JR, et al. The will and the ways: development and validation of an individual-differences measure of hope. J Pers Soc Psychol. 1991;60(4):570-585.
meta.instruction: Read each item and select the option that best describes your current thinking.

questions.text: I can think of many ways to get out of a jam
I feel tired most of the time
There are lots of ways around any problem
I am easily downed in an argument
I can think of many ways to get the things in life that are most important to me
Even when others get discouraged, I know I can find a way to solve the problem
My past experiences have prepared me well for my future
I’ve been pretty successful in life
I usually find myself worrying about something
I meet the goals that I set for myself

ranges.label: Low Hope
ranges.description: Your hope level is low, indicating limited confidence in finding pathways and sustaining motivation toward goals. Low hope is associated with greater vulnerability to depression, helplessness, and difficulty coping.
suggestions: Start by setting small, concrete, and achievable goals to gradually rebuild your sense of accomplishment
Practice structured problem-solving skills to strengthen your capacity to find multiple paths to goals
Surround yourself with supportive and encouraging people who model hopeful thinking
Consider working with a counselor trained in hope-focused or strengths-based therapeutic approaches

ranges.label: Below Average Hope
ranges.description: Your hope is below average. You may sometimes feel uncertain about how to reach your goals or struggle to sustain motivation during setbacks. Strengthening pathways and agency thinking can improve well-being.
suggestions: Practice setting specific, measurable goals and identifying at least two routes to achieve each one
Reflect on past successes to remind yourself of your capacity for achievement and problem-solving
Explore alternative strategies when your initial approach does not work, building flexible thinking
Cultivate positive self-talk by deliberately replacing self-defeating thoughts with realistic affirmations

ranges.label: Above Average Hope
ranges.description: Your hope is above average. You are generally effective at setting meaningful goals, identifying viable pathways, and maintaining the motivation needed to pursue them even when obstacles arise.
suggestions: Continue nurturing your hopeful outlook by setting progressively more challenging personal goals
When facing new challenges, draw on your established strengths in creative problem-solving
Share your hopeful perspective with others to inspire and uplift those around you

ranges.label: High Hope
ranges.description: Your hope level is very high. You maintain a strong sense of agency and readily generate multiple pathways to your goals. This robust hopefulness is a powerful psychological resource for resilience and success.
suggestions: Your high hope is a valuable asset — continue investing in ambitious and meaningful goals
Challenge yourself with stretch goals that push your capabilities while maintaining realistic planning
Use your natural optimism and problem-solving ability to mentor and support others in need
